purpose: Section-specific generate prompt for assigning impact_level (Critical / High / Medium / Low). Composed at runtime with system_prompts/generate.json.
system_addendum:
SECTION YOU ARE AUTHORING: impact_level

impact_level captures the blast radius of the failure mode the principle prevents. Drives criticality-tier filtering.

VALUES (from taxonomy.json enums.impact_level)

- **Critical** — failure produces immediate, severe, or irreversible harm: regulator action, large financial loss, customer-data exposure, safety incident. Adopters in regulated industries must have this principle in place day-one.
- **High** — failure produces material harm visible to customers or executives: customer trust erosion, repeated incident cycles, sustained productivity loss. Catalogue's most common level for foundational principles.
- **Medium** — failure produces friction or inefficiency observed mainly within the team: longer incident MTTR, slower onboarding, increased technical debt. Important but not existential.
- **Low** — failure produces local annoyance only: missed best-practice opportunity, mild tech debt, no customer-visible impact.

The four siblings (GO1B1-01..04) are all 'High' — the eval-discipline failure modes produce customer regressions, finance flags, audit findings, but not immediate safety/regulator events.

DIMENSIONS YOUR DRAFT WILL BE SCORED ON

1. value_in_enum — exactly Critical / High / Medium / Low.
2. blast_radius_consistent — the chosen level matches the failure mode described in the principle's problem section. A 'Critical' level implies a failure that reaches regulators or customers in irreversible ways; 'Low' implies local team friction.
3. parallel_with_siblings — within a BP family, principles tend to cluster at one level (foundational disciplines are typically High). Outliers should have a clear reason.

COMMON FAILURE MODES

- 'Critical' assigned when the failure is High at most. Inflation is the most common error.
- 'Low' assigned to a principle whose absence would actually produce customer-visible regressions. Under-estimation.
user_template:
Assign the impact_level for this principle.

<principle_id>{{ principle_id }}</principle_id>

<statement>
  <title>{{ statement_title }}</title>
</statement>

<problem>
  <description>{{ problem_description }}</description>
  <examples>{{ problem_examples }}</examples>
</problem>

<siblings_impact_levels>
{{ sibling_impact_levels }}
</siblings_impact_levels>

Return the JSON object specified in the output contract.

schema.impact_level: string — one of: Critical / High / Medium / Low
